title: Fried Chicken Sandwiches
description: From Barefoot Contessa Cook Like a Pro — the centerpiece of Ina's Summer Dinner family menu. Buttermilk-brined chicken thighs, dredged in a spiced flour coating and fried to golden perfection, then piled on a brioche bun with coleslaw and pickles. These are the best fried chicken sandwiches you'll ever make at home.
instructions: Brine the chicken: Combine the buttermilk, 1 tablespoon salt, and hot sauce in a large bowl. Add the chicken thighs, cover, and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Make the coating: Combine the flour, 1 tablespoon salt, pepper, garlic powder, paprika, and cayenne in a shallow bowl.
Remove the chicken from the brine, letting the excess drip off. Dredge each piece in the flour mixture, pressing firmly so it adheres. Let rest on a wire rack for 10 minutes.

Heat 1 inch of vegetable oil in a large (12-inch) cast iron skillet to 350°F. Fry the chicken in batches (don't crowd the pan) for 4 to 5 minutes per side, until deep golden brown and cooked through (165°F). Drain on a wire rack.
Spread mayonnaise on the toasted buns. Place a piece of fried chicken on each bottom bun, then top with pickles and coleslaw. Serve immediately.
tips: Overnight brining in buttermilk makes the chicken incredibly juicy and tender — plan ahead.
Letting the breaded chicken rest on a rack for 10 minutes before frying helps the coating adhere.
Use a thermometer to maintain the oil at 350°F — too hot burns the outside before the inside cooks.
Chicken thighs are better than breasts here — more flavorful and harder to overcook.
Source: Barefoot Contessa Cook Like a Pro by Ina Garten (2018)
